rules.rule:
REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; lead with the most serious problem and don't soften it. Verify every claim against the actual diff -- confirm function names, signatures, line numbers, and APIs before asserting; never invent behavior or cite code not present in the change. If unsure, say so instead of guessing, and tag each finding's confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no validating the author, no disclaimers; accuracy is the only success metric. Judge the change on its merits regardless of how it is framed. This is the Dynomite Rust port (a functional reimplementation of Netflix Dynomite); the conventions live in AGENTS.md and PLAN.md and are NON-NEGOTIABLE.

(1) NO STUBS: reject any unimplemented!(), todo!(), panic!("TODO"), or a function body that is a placeholder. Incomplete work must not be merged.

(2) NO LINT SUPPRESSION: reject any new #[allow(...)] that silences a warning unless it is justified in docs/journal/allowances.md (the change must reference that entry). Fix the cause, do not silence the lint. clippy::pedantic is on at the workspace root; module_name_repetitions and must_use_candidate are the only blanket relaxations.

(3) NO PORT-ACKNOWLEDGEMENT COMMENTS: reject comments like 'ported from C', 'matches dyn_xxx.c', 'from the C source', or similar. The Rust code reads as first-class Rust; acknowledgement of the upstream C project lives ONLY in README.md, NOTICE, LICENSE, CHANGELOG, and docs/parity.md.

(4) ASCII ONLY: reject any non-ASCII character in code, comments, or strings -- including smart quotes, em-dashes, single-char ellipses, arrows, and accented letters. The only exception is intentional non-ASCII payload bytes in a .bin test fixture.

(5) NO UNSAFE: lib.rs carries #![forbid(unsafe_code)]. Any unsafe must be isolated in a single module with #![allow(unsafe_code)] AND a module-level Safety doc section; flag any unsafe that lacks both.

(6) ERROR HANDLING: typed errors via thiserror at module boundaries; anyhow::Result only in the binary's main. Reject .unwrap() outside #[cfg(test)] code; .expect("...") is allowed only when the invariant is documented in the same function's docs. Reject swallowed errors (let _ = on a Result that can fail meaningfully, ignored Result from a fallible call).

(7) NO GLOBAL MUTABLE STATE outside core::context::Context. No lazy_static; use OnceLock/OnceCell.

(8) PARITY: when behavior maps to a documented C function, confirm the Rust reproduces the C behavior rather than 'improving' it; any deliberate change must be a Deviation entry in docs/parity.md. Watch for off-by-one in mbuf indexing, operator precedence, integer overflow, and token/ring arithmetic.

(9) RISK: flag panics on attacker-controlled input, unbounded resource use, deadlocks, lock-ordering hazards, cancellation-unsafe async, and integer overflow. Public items must have rustdoc with a doctested # Examples section.

REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; lead with the most serious problem and don't soften it. Verify every claim against the actual diff -- confirm function names, signatures, line numbers, and APIs before asserting; never invent behavior or cite code not present in the change. If unsure, say so instead of guessing, and tag each finding's confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no validating the author, no disclaimers; accuracy is the only success metric. Dynomite tests. .unwrap()/.expect() are acceptable in test code. Confirm the test class fits the job (unit for single-function contracts, regression in tests/ for multi-module flows, property via hegeltest for invariants, fuzz for arbitrary-bytes-no-panic). Each fixed bug should get a permanent regression_<desc> test linking to the parity entry or commit. Property tests must run >= 256 cases. Parsers must be exercised against arbitrary bytes and never panic. Flag tests that touch the network in the unit tier, tests that assert on nondeterministic timing, and 'coverage padding' tests that assert nothing meaningful.

REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; lead with the most serious problem and don't soften it. Verify every claim against the actual diff before asserting; tag confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no filler. Dynomite cargo-fuzz harness. Mandatory targets: proto_redis_parse, proto_memcache_parse, dnode_parse, conf_parse, crypto_aes_decrypt (fixed key). A fuzz target must drive a real parser/decoder and must not itself panic on malformed input by construction (the harness exercises the code under test, not asserts on it). Corpus/artifact dirs stay gitignored; only curated seeds under crates/fuzz/seeds/ are committed.

REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; verify every claim against the diff; tag confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no filler. Dynomite Cargo/config manifests. New dependencies are an UPPER-BOUND violation unless PLAN.md Section 2 is updated and a journal entry approves the addition -- flag any new dependency that lacks that. Confirm clippy::pedantic lints stay configured at the workspace root and per-crate [lints]. rustfmt settings: imports_granularity = "Crate", group_imports = "StdExternalCrate". Workspace builds must stay --locked-clean; flag a Cargo.lock change with no corresponding manifest change rationale. Pin and feature changes should be intentional and minimal.

REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; verify every claim against the diff; tag confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no filler. Dynomite GitHub Actions / CI config. scripts/check.sh is the single source of truth for 'green CI'; workflows are thin wrappers that invoke it. A new gate belongs in scripts/check.sh, NOT inlined into a workflow. Flag hardcoded secrets (auth must be GitHub OIDC, no static AWS keys), over-broad token permissions (default to least privilege: contents: read; widen only where the job posts), unpinned third-party actions where a SHA/tag is expected, and shell that runs untrusted PR input. The GitHub workflow and the Forgejo mirror (.forgejo/) must stay in sync where both apply.

REVIEW DISCIPLINE: Be precise and blunt; verify every claim against the diff; tag confidence (high/moderate/low). No praise, no filler. Dynomite docs (Markdown / mdBook). ASCII only -- no smart quotes, em-dashes, single-char ellipses, or arrows. Clear heading hierarchy; fenced code blocks with language hints; accurate instructions and prerequisites; no broken relative links or stale claims; consistent terminology with AGENTS.md/PLAN.md. docs/parity.md is the C-to-Rust mapping and ambiguity log; changes there must keep the symbol mapping accurate. New user-facing behavior in code should come with matching doc changes. The Netflix C origin is acknowledged only in README/NOTICE/LICENSE/CHANGELOG/parity.md.
